event_investment_decisions.event_description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's October 2025 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for October 28 - 29, 2025 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their October meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

decision.rationale: Recent Fed policy data shows the FOMC cut rates by 25 bps on Sept 17 and futures now price an 86% chance of another cut at the October meeting. The market currently prices a Yes (25 bps cut) at 0.785, implying a 78.5% probability. This is significantly below the futures‑derived 86% odds, meaning the market is undervaluing the likelihood of a 25‑bps reduction. I therefore place a positive bet on the Yes outcome.

market_investment_decisions.market_question: Fed decreases interest rates by 25 bps after October 2025 meeting?

decision.rationale: Futures indicate an 86% chance of a cut, leaving only about a 14% chance of no change. The market prices a Yes (no change) at 0.145, i.e., a 14.5% implied probability, which is slightly higher than the futures‑derived odds. I assess the true probability of no change to be nearer 10%, so the market is modestly overvaluing the no‑change outcome. I therefore short the Yes outcome (bet on No).

decision.rationale: The NRFI (No Run First Inning) market currently prices the "Yes" outcome at 0.9955, implying a 99.55% chance that neither team scores in the first inning. The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's October 2025 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for October 28 - 29, 2025 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their October meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

decision.rationale: The market currently prices a 78.5% chance that the Fed will cut rates by 25 basis points after the October 2025 meeting. Recent Fed communications (FedWatch tool) and the September meeting outcome (a 25bp cut) indicate that the probability of another 25bp cut is even higher, around 94% according to the latest fed funds futures and dot‑plot expectations. The market therefore underestimates the likelihood of a cut, making a positive (Yes) bet attractive.
